primary.title: Compound Interest Calculator - Calculate Savings Growth
primary.description: Calculate how your money grows over time with compound interest. Compare compound intervals and project your future wealth.
primary.heading: Compound Interest Calculator
primary.seo_content:
Our Compound Interest Calculator computes future values using compounding or simple calculations. Here is the reference guide:

  • 1. Compound Interest (Compounding Growth)

    Formula: A = P × (1 + r/n)^(n×t) (without contributions).
    Where: P = Principal, r = Annual rate, n = Compounding frequency, t = Years.
    Example: Investing $10,000 at 8% compounded monthly for 10 years yields a total balance of $22,196.40.

  • 2. Simple Interest (Linear Growth)

    Formula: Interest = P × r × t
    Example: A $10,000 principal at 8% simple interest for 10 years earns exactly $8,000.00 in interest.

examples.title: Maturity of a $10,000 investment
examples.description: Investing $10,000 at an 8% annual interest rate compounded monthly for 10 years results in a total balance of $22,196.40, earning $12,196.40 in interest.

examples.title: Regular monthly contributions
examples.description: Starting with $1,000 and contributing $200 each month at a 7% interest rate compounded monthly for 20 years results in a final portfolio of $105,429.94.

faqs.question: What is compound interest?
faqs.answer: Compound interest is the interest calculated on the initial principal and also on the accumulated interest of previous periods. It allows investments to grow at an accelerating rate over time.

faqs.question: How does compound frequency affect returns?
faqs.answer: The more frequently interest is compounded, the higher the total return. For example, compounding monthly yields slightly more than compounding annually, and daily compounding yields the most.

compound-savings-calculator.title: Compound Savings Calculator - Estimate Savings Growth
compound-savings-calculator.description: Estimate future growth of your savings account with compound rates and monthly deposits. Free savings compounding planner.
compound-savings-calculator.heading: Compound Savings Calculator
compound-savings-calculator.seo_content:
The Compound Savings Calculator lets you calculate and visualize how regular monthly contributions combined with interest compounding accelerate your path to your savings goals. Here is a projection example:

  • Maturity growth with deposits

    Example: Starting with $5,000 and contributing $500 monthly at 5% interest rate compounded monthly for 5 years yields $39,761.35 total balance.

examples.title: Saving $500 a month
examples.description: Starting with $5,000 and saving $500 monthly at 5% compounded monthly for 5 years yields $39,761.35, with over $4,700 in interest.

faqs.question: What is the difference between APR and APY?
faqs.answer: APR (Annual Percentage Rate) does not account for compounding within the year. APY (Annual Percentage Yield) represents the actual annual return including the effect of compounding.

interest-calculator.title: Interest Calculator - Find Accrued Investment Returns
interest-calculator.description: Calculate interest returns on loans, deposits, and fixed savings assets. Free compound interest tools.
interest-calculator.heading: Interest Calculator
interest-calculator.seo_content:
Calculate interest returns on deposits or fixed savings assets. Here is how simple interest compares to compound interest:

  • Simple Interest Accumulation

    Formula: Interest = Principal × Rate × Years
    Example: A $5,000 deposit at 6% simple interest for 3 years earns exactly $900.00 interest.

examples.title: Simple interest comparison
examples.description: A $5,000 deposit at 6% simple interest for 3 years earns exactly $900 in total interest ($300 per year).

faqs.question: How is simple interest different from compound interest?
faqs.answer: Simple interest is calculated only on the original principal. Compound interest calculates interest on the principal plus all interest already earned in prior periods.
